rows.notes: Clear centered year at the top of the page, legible and unambiguous.
Clear section title (underlined) immediately under the year; fully legible.
All three columns (7 | 3 | 9) are well aligned with the vertical rules and clearly written.
Pounds (2) and shillings (11) are legible; the pence column is blank/dash. Chosen to leave pence empty rather than assume 0.

rows.description: Surridge. viz to Octr 29. 1855. by Wm L
rows.notes: Pounds 8 and shillings 5 clearly visible; pence column blank. Date and 'by Wm L' legible enough for description.
Pounds figure '6' is visible; the shillings and pence columns look blank or faint. Some ink fade around the narrow columns makes shillings/pence uncertain.
Pounds 3 and shillings 14 are well aligned and legible; pence column blank. Small flourish on the 14 checked against vertical guides.
Amounts read as 3 | 12 | (blank). Columns are clear and values are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Collins & Hunt. Remainder of Contract Manor Farm
rows.notes: Pounds appears to be '14' but the shillings/pence columns are faint or absent. The wordiness of the description is clear; amount alignment slightly uncertain.
All three columns (6 | 17 | 6) are crisp and align well with the ruled columns; values validate (shillings <20, pence <12).
Clear three-column entry (21 | 9 | 6). Handwriting and alignment make this unambiguous.
Large, very clear amounts (76 | 18 | 3) aligned to the column rules; high contrast ink makes reading certain.
Small but clear three-column figure (1 | 4 | 2). Column boundaries used to confirm digits.

rows.description: Mr Walsh. Expenses on transfer & the sale of 2 1000 bonds from Exors of late Jno Jones. Order April 25. 1855
rows.notes: Pounds 20 and shillings 10 are legible and align with the pounds/shillings lines; pence column appears blank. The long descriptive text is partly abbreviated in the original but readable.

rows.description: Fanshawe. many library Books, see fol other Decr 11th 1854
rows.notes: A clear '10' in the pounds column; the narrower shillings/pence columns are blank. The note referring to Decr 11th is legible.
Single '3' in pounds column clearly visible; no smaller values in shillings/pence.

rows.description: Terry Barn. Straw & carriage for thatching
rows.notes: Pounds 19 and shillings 5 are clear; pence column blank. Slight ink flourish on the 19 but alignment confirms reading.
Pounds read as '40' and fairly distinct; the shillings/pence columns are blank or too faint to read. The surname could be 'Benson' or 'Benton' but 'Benson' fits preceding usage.
Three-column amount (7 | 10 | 3) is visible and aligns well with the ruled lines; description clearly refers to expenses for new trustees.
Pounds looks like '12' and sits correctly in the pounds column; shillings/pence are blank or faint. The abbreviation 'Rep:' interpreted as 'Repairs' or 'Rept'.

rows.description: Tweed Journey to London on Coll. acct
rows.notes: Reads as 1 pound and 15 shillings. The leading '1' is small; alignment with the shillings column confirms '15'. Pence blank.

rows.description: Honor. work done on Seckham Farm allc
rows.notes: Pounds '15' is visible but the description has abbreviations and the smaller amount columns are blank. Some ink fade on this line reduces certainty.
Clear three-column figure (4 | 12 | 6) with good alignment. Handwriting of numerals is unambiguous.

rows.description: Miss Jones Int. on 1000 from College
rows.notes: Pounds '14' readable; shillings/pence blank. 'Int.' interpreted as interest; 'College' reading inferred from context though partly abbreviated.
Three-column figures (53 | 4 | 5) are fairly clear though the leading '5' of 53 has a slight flourish; validation checks OK.
Pounds '59' visible in the pounds column; smaller columns appear blank/faint. The surname matches earlier 'Benson' entries.
Reads as 14 | 17 | (blank). Shillings '17' is clear; pence blank. Description is short and legible.

rows.description: Faulkner. South Newton allowed for Gates
rows.notes: Three-column entry (9 | 10 | 6) cleanly written and aligned. 'South Newton' and 'Gates' legible in the description.
Amounts 4 | 5 | 10 are clear. The description uses abbreviation 'Rep:' and a short form 'Surance' for assurance; reading matches the script style elsewhere.
Pounds 3 and shillings 7 are faint but aligned; pence blank. Surname 'Kimber' and 'Little Sen.' abbreviated in original and slightly unclear.
Pounds 6 and shillings 1 visible; pence column blank. 'Keldlington' reading relies on local place-name spelling in the ledger (slight ink flourish).
Pounds 5 and shillings 6 clear and align with ruled columns; pence blank. Description is legible.

rows.description: Dr Glayice. copy of Papers on deck = Univ's Archives
rows.notes: Pounds '1' visible; smaller columns blank. The name 'Dr Glayice' and 'Univ's Archives' are somewhat abbreviated and faint but plausible from the script.
Final line with a clear three-column total (19 | 13 | 10) aligned to the ruled columns. Description is legible and looks like the concluding allowance entry.
